spec_match.rationale: The demo now teaches the correct CSS Images 4 fallback forms: image(<url>, <color>) for one source and image-set(...), image(<color>) as separate background layers.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the color picker and confirmed the generated CSS plus preview background fallbacks update live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede matches the repaired page: solid-color generated images for placeholders, URL fallback examples, and branded slot fillers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now warns when native image(<color>) parsing is unsupported and keeps visible background-color fallbacks instead of showing blank preview cards.

references.rationale: Includes the required ChromeStatus entry plus MDN and CSS Images Level 4 references.

design_system.rationale: The added status panel and previews use shared CSS variables, avoid inline event handlers, and keep contrast readable.

summary: Re-reviewed in chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148.0.0.0 at /v150/css-image-color-function/gradient-fallback/?verify=after-code-example-fix. Before the fix, the visible sample placed image(oklch(...)) inside image-set() and implied network fallback behavior that image-set() does not provide. After the fix, the generated and static samples use separate background layers and the explicit image("avatar.png", color) fallback form; the old sample text is absent. The color picker updated the generated CSS and fallback background colors; the page displayed an unsupported-browser note because Chrome 148 does not parse image(<color>) yet. Console was empty, page network requests completed successfully, and the updated /v150/css-image-color-function/conformance/?verify=after-gradient-fallback-fix route ran 8 assertions with the expected Chrome 148 unsupported result of 1 PASS / 7 FAIL.
